# [recovery-daemon] Rung-2 via B0 — supervised-process restart (privilege-free)

**Parent:** Epic #13874. Graduated from Discussion #13871 (Axis B → B0).

## Problem / scope
A large share of "crash/hang" faults are **orchestrator-supervised** processes (the local model server, embeddings server, vector store) that `ProcessSupervisorService` already restarts past a cooldown — zero new privilege. B0 makes the recovery daemon ACT on the `crash — supervised process` class without the docker socket, shrinking the privileged surface to the external-container case only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Rung-2 via B0:** restart an orchestrator-supervised process via `ProcessSupervisorService.superviseTask` + `RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS` (the `crash — supervised process` cell).
- [ ] Escalates to Rung-3 (redeploy/page) if it restart-loops past N (anti-thrash, persisted).
- [ ] Covers the restart half of the `exhaustion` cell (Rung-0 shed + Rung-2 restart).
- [ ] Selects B0 (vs external) deterministically from `targetIdentity.kind === 'supervised-task'` (the C4 contract) — no inference.
- [ ] Observability + verify-loop inherited from the daemon-core sub.
- [ ] **Zero new privilege** (AC-7 composition).

## Contract Ledger
- Adds: the B0 actuator binding + the `crash — supervised process` / `exhaustion` policy cells.
- Reuses: `ProcessSupervisorService.superviseTask`, `RESTART_COOLDOWN_MS`.

## Sequencing
Blocked-by the daemon-core sub (daemon + dispatch). Privilege-free. **Fire-relief priority** — covers a supervised local-model-server crash.

## Routing update — blocked by daemon-core/fire-relief sub

Live pickup re-check on 2026-06-23T02:38Z found this B0 supervised-process restart leaf valid, but not implementation-ready as an independent lane yet.

Current dependency chain:
- #13882 is the daemon-core / fire-relief implementation slice and is still open through PR `#13900`.
- #13900 currently contains the running-child `healthProbe` recycle path and is still under review (`CHANGES_REQUESTED` state at the latest live poll).
- This ticket's generalized B0 policy cell should be implemented against the landed daemon-core substrate, not raced in parallel against a moving recovery surface.

I applied `not-code-ready` + `deferred-by-design` so this does not keep surfacing as an ordinary unassigned code lane while `#13900` is unresolved. The GitHub relationship write was rejected by the issue-relations API (`Target issue has already been taken`), so this comment carries the revalidation trigger instead.

Revalidation trigger: when #13882 closes via `#13900` or a successor PR, remove the readiness labels and re-intake #13883 against current `dev`, especially the persisted anti-thrash / Rung-3 escalation ACs that #13900 explicitly scopes out for B0.

## Revalidation — #13882 landed, B0 sub is code-ready

The prior blocker on this leaf is resolved:

- #13882 is closed as of 2026-06-23T06:49:17Z via the merged #13900 line.
- #13880 and #13881 are already closed, so the ADR/contract side this B0 leaf depends on is present.
- #13883 was still carrying `not-code-ready` + `deferred-by-design` from the pre-#13900 holding comment; those labels are now stale and have been removed.

I assigned #13883 to @neo-gpt and am re-intaking it against current `dev`. Scope stays narrow: privilege-free supervised-process restart policy via `targetIdentity.kind === 'supervised-task'`, persisted anti-thrash / Rung-3 escalation evidence, and no docker/deploy actuator work here.

#13884 remains sequenced after this leaf because it is the privileged B1 / Rung-3 actuator slice.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-23T08:34:23Z

## Superseded by ADR-0026 / #13900

Grace Cycle 2 on #13912 correctly caught that this ticket AC set drifted after ADR-0026 landed. The B0 supervised-process recovery slice is now explicitly narrowed to the supervisor in-process cooldown, and that live B0 path is already shipped by #13900 / #13882. Durable anti-thrash and the controller-agnostic `apply(serviceKey, action)` seam belong to the remaining B1 / privileged-actuator lane (#13884), not a second B0 service.

Closing this ticket as superseded rather than iterating the wrong-shape PR. Recovery-daemon sequencing should continue through #13884 against ADR-0026.

Related closure: #13912 closed as Drop+Supersede.
